channel.title: Shanios Blog
channel.description: Engineering breakdowns, release notes, and stories from the team building India

item.title: shani-update — The Shani OS Update Manager
item.description: shani-update is the user-facing update manager for Shani OS. It runs automatically via a systemd user timer and at login, detects boot failures, checks for staged updates awaiting a reboot, identifies newly deployed slots, and checks for new OS images — all with GUI dialogs, automatic terminal detection, and a deferred-reminder system.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Adding LUKS2 Encryption After Installation on Shani OS
item.description: Did not enable LUKS2 encryption during installation? This guide walks through adding full-disk encryption to an existing unencrypted Shani OS installation — backing up data, repartitioning with LUKS2, restoring, and enrolling TPM2 for passwordless unlock.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Shani OS vs Fedora Silverblue, Bazzite, SteamOS, and NixOS — An Honest Comparison
item.description: An honest technical comparison of immutable Linux distributions. Where Shani OS wins, where others genuinely win, and how to pick the right one for your situation. Btrfs send/receive vs OSTree vs OCI images, gaming focus, enterprise needs, and declarative vs imperative philosophy.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Running Windows Apps on Shani OS — Wine, Bottles, and When to Use a VM
item.description: You do not need Windows to run Windows software on Shani OS. Bottles (pre-installed on the KDE Plasma edition) manages Wine environments for productivity tools, creative software, and legacy apps. Here is how to get any Windows app running, and when a VM is the better answer.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Apptainer (Singularity) on Shani OS — HPC Containers for Workstation and Cluster
item.description: Apptainer is the HPC standard for portable, reproducible containers — pre-installed on Shani OS, no root required, runs the same SIF image on your workstation and on any SLURM/PBS cluster. Build once, run anywhere, with full NVIDIA and AMD ROCm GPU support.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: GPU Compute on Shani OS — NVIDIA CUDA, AMD ROCm, and Intel oneAPI
item.description: How to run GPU compute workloads on Shani OS — setting up AMD ROCm with PyTorch and TensorFlow via Distrobox, NVIDIA CUDA via Distrobox, Intel oneAPI, HIP for cross-vendor portability, and Nix for pure ML environments. Works for ML, simulation, data science, and scientific computing.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: LXC and LXD on Shani OS — Full System Containers
item.description: LXC and LXD run full Linux system containers on Shani OS — complete OS environments sharing the host kernel, stored in dedicated @lxc and @lxd Btrfs subvolumes that survive every OS update. Lighter than VMs, more complete than Distrobox.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: systemd-nspawn and machinectl on Shani OS — Lightweight System Containers
item.description: systemd-nspawn is the lightest way to run a full Linux system environment on Shani OS — no image management, no daemon, no setup. Containers boot in under a second, live in the @machines Btrfs subvolume, and survive every OS update untouched.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: System Configuration on Shani OS — /etc, Services, and What Persists
item.description: The /etc OverlayFS on Shani OS means all your configuration changes persist normally across every OS update and rollback. This guide covers editing config files, managing systemd services, enabling services, kernel parameters, and inspecting what you have customised.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Audio on Shani OS — PipeWire, WirePlumber, JACK, and Professional Audio
item.description: Shani OS uses PipeWire 1.4.x as its audio stack — simultaneously compatible with ALSA, PulseAudio, and JACK. This guide covers everyday audio management, Bluetooth codec selection, fixing common issues, and setting up low-latency professional audio workflows.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: KDE Connect and GSConnect on Shani OS — Your Phone and Desktop, Together
item.description: KDE Connect (KDE edition) and GSConnect (GNOME edition) link your Android phone to your Shani OS desktop — notifications mirrored, files shared, clipboard synced, remote input, SMS from desktop, and media control. Firewall rules are pre-configured.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Indian Language Support on Shani OS — Devanagari, Tamil, Telugu, and More
item.description: Indian language support on Shani OS is designed in from the start — not bolted on. Devanagari, Tamil, Telugu, Kannada, Malayalam, Gujarati, Punjabi, Bengali, and more: correct font rendering, IBus multilingual input, and Indian-English locale all configured at first boot.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: AppImage on Shani OS — Portable Apps with Gear Lever
item.description: AppImages are self-contained portable executables that need no installation and work on any Linux system. Gear Lever (pre-installed) integrates them into your desktop launcher, manages updates, and keeps them in persistent storage that survives OS updates.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Snap on Shani OS — The Optional App Ecosystem
item.description: Snap is pre-configured on Shani OS with its own @snapd Btrfs subvolume. When to use Snap versus Flatpak, how to install from the Snap Store, and how the two ecosystems coexist.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: gen-efi and Secure Boot on Shani OS — UKI Generation, MOK Enrollment, and TPM2
item.description: gen-efi is the tool that builds and signs Unified Kernel Images, manages MOK keys for Secure Boot, and enrolls LUKS keys into TPM2. This is the complete reference for every gen-efi operation and the Secure Boot chain on Shani OS.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Accessibility on Shani OS — Screen Reader, Braille, Magnification, and More
item.description: Shani OS ships a complete accessibility stack at first boot — Orca screen reader with espeak-ng TTS, braille display support via brltty, display magnification, high-contrast themes, keyboard navigation, and assistive technology infrastructure. Nothing to install.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Homebrew on Shani OS — For macOS Switchers and Those Who Prefer It
item.description: Homebrew works on Shani OS exactly as it does on macOS — install it once, use brew install for any of the thousands of formulae. A comparison with Nix to help you choose, and how both coexist.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Btrfs Snapshots and Backup on Shani OS — Protecting Your Data the Right Way
item.description: How to use Btrfs snapshots for instant point-in-time copies of your home directory, and how to set up real off-device backups with restic and rclone — both pre-installed on Shani OS. Snapshots are not backups. Here is the difference and how to do both.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: shani-deploy Reference — Every Flag, Every Workflow
item.description: The complete reference for shani-deploy — the atomic update and rollback tool at the heart of Shani OS. Every flag, every use case, every edge case covered: update, rollback, dry-run, cleanup, optimize, channel management, and more.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Bluetooth on Shani OS — Headphones, Controllers, File Transfer, and Pairing
item.description: Bluetooth on Shani OS works at first boot for audio, gaming controllers, keyboards, mice, file transfer, and tethering. Paired devices persist across every OS update and rollback. Here is everything you need to know.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Printing and Scanning on Shani OS — CUPS, Driverless Printing, and SANE
item.description: How printing and scanning work on Shani OS — driverless IPP-over-USB and network discovery, CUPS configuration, manufacturer-specific drivers for HP, Epson, Brother, Canon, and scanner setup via SANE and sane-airscan.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Power Management on Shani OS — Hibernation, Suspend, Battery Life, and Profiles
item.description: Shani OS configures hibernation automatically at install, manages power profiles out of the box, and handles TPM2-sealed LUKS so the disk unlocks silently on your own hardware. Everything a laptop user needs — with nothing to configure.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: The Shell Experience on Shani OS — Zsh, Starship, McFly, FZF, and More
item.description: Shani OS ships a fully configured, modern shell environment — Zsh with syntax highlighting and autosuggestions, Starship prompt, McFly neural-network history, FZF fuzzy search, and a complete toolkit of CLI tools. Here is how it all fits together.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Podman and Containers on Shani OS — Rootless, Docker-Compatible, Ready at Boot
item.description: How to use Podman on Shani OS for rootless containers, Docker Compose workflows, and containerised services — with the Pods GUI, persistent storage in @containers, and full compatibility with the Docker ecosystem.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Distrobox on Shani OS — A Full Mutable Linux Inside an Immutable One
item.description: How to use Distrobox to run any Linux distribution inside Shani OS — full pacman, apt, dnf, AUR helpers, and GUI apps — without touching the immutable host OS. With BoxBuddy for graphical management.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Migrating to Shani OS from Ubuntu, Fedora, or Arch — A Practical Guide
item.description: A workflow-by-workflow migration guide for users coming from traditional mutable Linux distributions. Every pacman, apt, and dnf habit has a clean equivalent on Shani OS — here is the full map.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Running Android Apps on Shani OS with Waydroid
item.description: Waydroid runs a full hardware-accelerated Android stack inside a lightweight container on Shani OS — full Play Store access, ARM app compatibility, and native GPU performance. Here is how to set it up and get the most out of it.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Flatpak on Shani OS — Your Complete App Management Guide
item.description: How Flatpak works on Shani OS, what apps are pre-installed, how to install more from Flathub, manage permissions with Flatseal, and why sandboxing is the right model for an immutable system.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Shani OS for Researchers and HPC — Reproducible Environments from Desktop to Cluster
item.description: How Shani OS makes scientific computing reproducible from the ground up — Apptainer for cluster workloads, Nix for pinned environments, AMD ROCm and NVIDIA CUDA via Distrobox containers, DVC for data versioning, and a GPG-signed host OS that is itself a verifiable artefact.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Gaming on Shani OS — The Full Stack, Explained
item.description: Everything pre-installed on the KDE Plasma edition and why it matters: Steam + Proton, MangoHud, GameScope, Ananicy, kernel tuning, NVIDIA setup, peripheral support, and why immutability makes Shani OS the most reliable gaming OS on Linux.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Shani OS for OEMs and IT Fleets — Deploy Once, Maintain Never
item.description: How Shani OS eliminates per-device drift, enables one-command remote rollback, automates firmware updates, and makes fleet management genuinely simple — whether you are an OEM shipping hardware or an IT team managing hundreds of machines.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Virtual Machines on Shani OS — QEMU/KVM, virt-manager, GPU Passthrough, and Windows VMs
item.description: How to run fast, hardware-accelerated VMs on Shani OS — setting up KVM with virt-manager, running Windows with VirtIO drivers, GPU passthrough for gaming or ML, and why the @libvirt subvolume keeps your VMs safe across every OS update.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Shani OS 2026.04.15 — Release Notes
item.description: The April 2026 stable release: fixed UEFI installer, improved shani-deploy reliability, refreshed GNOME 50 and KDE Plasma 6 ISOs, and a full package manifest.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Security Without Configuration — How Shani OS Protects You by Default
item.description: Six Linux Security Modules running simultaneously, an immutable root that survives root access, TPM2-sealed encryption, and zero telemetry. Here is exactly how each layer works and why it is on by default.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Shani OS for Everyone — Gamers, Developers, Switchers, and IT Teams
item.description: A practical guide to who Shani OS is for and what it looks like in daily use — whether you are coming from Windows, running a game library, managing a fleet, or doing serious dev work.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: The Architecture Behind Shani OS — Subvolumes, Slots, and the Update Pipeline
item.description: A detailed walkthrough of how Shani OS is structured on disk — every Btrfs subvolume, every mount, every step from image signing to your running desktop.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Getting Started with Shani OS — From Download to Daily Use
item.description: A complete walkthrough: download, verify, install, and configure Shani OS from scratch — including Flatpak apps, Nix packages, Distrobox containers, and TPM2 encryption setup.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Why Your OS Update Should Never Break Your Computer — And How Shani OS Fixes This
item.description: Every server admin knows: you never update the live system. You update the standby, verify it, then switch. Desktops never got this lesson — until now.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Nix on Shani OS — The Best Package Manager for an Immutable System
item.description: Why Nix and immutable Linux are a natural match, how to set it up on Shani OS in five minutes, and practical workflows for developers, researchers, and power users.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar

item.title: Networking on Shani OS — VPNs, Tailscale, SSH, Caddy, and More
item.description: A practical guide to every network capability built into Shani OS — from WireGuard and Tailscale mesh VPNs to Caddy web server, SSH hardening, remote desktop, and NFS file sharing.
item.author: Shrinivas Kumbhar
